Title: Medical image pretraining-based transfer learning for generalizable and robust diagnosis of bone tumors on radiographs: a multi-center study.
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: benign vs non-benign bone tumor
Data_Available: BTXRD dataset publicly available; internal on request
Notes: Internal test (primary): FM 0.785 vs Rad 0.694, diff 0.091. External test (secondary, n=1867): FM 0.738 vs Rad 0.518, diff 0.220 (RadImageNet advantage larger on external). Other ext AUCs: ResNet50_ImageNet 0.669, InceptionV3_RadImageNet 0.703, InceptionV3_ImageNet 0.677. 73 texture + 364 wavelet features. Grad-CAM interpretability.

Title: One novel transfer learning-based CLIP model combined with self-attention mechanism for differentiating the tumor-stroma ratio in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma.
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: high vs low tumor-stroma ratio (TSR)
FM_Pretraining: OpenAI CLIP (WIT image-text pairs, vision-language contrastive)
Stat_Comparison: NR (DeLong used for phase comparisons only, not model vs model)
Notes: Multi-phase (arterial+venous CT) fusion design. Other FM arms (test AUC): ResNet50 0.959, ResNet101 0.926, ViT-B/16 0.908. Best single-phase: venous CLIP-ViT-B/32 0.930. Kaplan-Meier overall survival p<0.05. No external validation.

Title: Noninvasive preoperative risk stratification of prostate cancer via a foundational model based deep learning with PSMA PET/CT.
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: ISUP Grade Group 1-2 vs 3-5
FM_Pretraining: PMC-15M (vision-language contrastive on 15M biomedical image-text pairs)
Texture_Families: GLCM, GLRLM, GLSZM, GLDM, NGTDM (PyRadiomics defaults)
Notes: Primary: pure BiomedCLIP-only arm AUC 0.764 (vs Radiomics/RF 0.676; diff 0.088). Hybrid BiomedCLIP+3D-ResNet-34 dual-path AUC 0.800 (not used as primary per protocol). Other FM baselines same cohort (5-fold CV): MedSAM 0.759, XSurv 0.756, ResNet 0.745. Class balance: 77.2% GG3-5 (imbalanced). SUVmax baseline AUC 0.699 (≥GG3) / 0.625 (≥GG4). Hybrid PRC-AUC 0.928 ± 0.020 across folds. Decision-curve analysis (DCA) favourable across wide thresholds. Single-centre retrospective (n=494, [18F]PSMA-1007 PET/CT). No external validation. META-ANALYSIS RECONCILIATION 2026-05-19: Cohort_Internal_Test cleaned from '494 (5-fold CV)' to just '494' (5-fold CV nature still documented in Phase 4a notes above). Cohort_Train cleaned from 'NR (5-fold CV split internally)' to 'NR'. This allows R script parse_n() to extract the cohort size correctly. 5-fold CV uses the same 494 patients in train+test across folds; Hanley-McNeil applied to n=494 (full cohort) per the user's chosen option.

Title: End-to-end prediction of clinical outcomes in head and neck squamous cell carcinoma with foundation model-based multiple instance learning.
Clinical_Task: Prognostic: 2-year overall survival (OS); also LRC and FFDM
Stat_Comparison: Permutation test p=0.012 (FM vs Rad on HN-PET-CT)
Notes: 3 test cohorts: RADCURE-test (internal, n=606), HN1 (external, n=131), HN-PET-CT (external, n=284). Primary value 0.84 from HN-PET-CT external (paired with Rad 0.68). OS AUCs across cohorts: FM RADCURE 0.75, HN1 0.77, HN-PET-CT 0.84. Multiple FM backbones tested (BiomedCLIP, ResNet50/ImageNet, SwinViT) with similar performance — best-performing 2D MIL backbone used as primary. Additional endpoints: LRC AUROC 0.66-0.75 (FM) vs 0.64-0.74 (Rad); FFDM AUROC 0.71-0.78 (FM) vs 0.64-0.74 (Rad). 2D MIL > 3D MIL > multiview MIL. Hazard ratios FM-stratified high-risk vs low-risk: 3.97-4.09 across cohorts. Multimodal enhancement of 2-year OS/FFDM in non-HPV+ patients (AUROC 0.82-0.87).

Title: Computed tomography-based deep-learning prediction of induction chemotherapy treatment response in locally advanced nasopharyngeal carcinoma.
Clinical_Task: Treatment response: induction chemotherapy responders vs non-responders
Notes: 6 pretrained CNN backbones tested on same 89-patient test cohort; ResNet50 (used as primary) best-performing. Test AUCs of alternative FM backbones: InceptionV3 0.757, Xception 0.668, IncResNetV2 0.640, VGG16 0.568, VGG19 0.534. Combined (FM + radiomics) model AUC 0.694 — LOWER than ResNet50 alone (fusion did not help). Clinical baseline AUC 0.572 (just above chance). No external validation.

Title: Combining multiparametric MRI features-based transfer learning and clinical parameters: application of machine learning for the differentiation of uterine sarcomas from atypical leiomyomas.
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: uterine sarcoma vs atypical leiomyoma (ALM)
Notes: Cohort: 86 sarcoma + 86 ALM (n=172 total), 7:3 train/test split. 4 ImageNet-pretrained CNN backbones tested on T2WI+DWI; ResNet50 selected as primary (best). Other T2-DWI FM AUCs: InceptionV3 0.89, InceptionResNetV2 0.85, Xception 0.85. Complex multiparametric model (FM + radiomics + clinical) gave FM 0.96 vs Rad 0.92 — slight FM advantage maintained even with clinical features added. Primary comparison reported here is pure FM (0.92) vs pure radiomics (0.79). No external validation.

Title: Prediction of lung malignancy progression and survival with machine learning based on pre-treatment FDG-PET/CT.
Clinical_Task: Prognostic: Overall Survival (also progression as auxiliary task)
FM_Usage_Mode: Fine-tune (pretrained then fine-tuned on progression task; penultimate-layer features extracted)
Texture_Families: First-order, Shape (2D/3D), GLCM, GLRLM, GLSZM, GLDM, NGTDM
Notes: 3 sites pooled (RIH/HUP/XSH); no external validation. CNN fine-tuned on progression task, then penultimate-layer features fed to RSF for OS prediction. Best FM arm: PET+CT ensemble with manual masks. Best radiomics arm: CT-only. Auxiliary task (progression prediction) achieved ensemble AUC 0.876 — FM advantage larger for binary progression task than for OS C-index. ComBAT harmonisation tested. FIRST PAPER IN CORPUS WHERE FM VS RADIOMICS DIFFERENCE IS NOT STATISTICALLY SIGNIFICANT — useful for heterogeneity discussion.

Title: Contrast-Enhanced CT-Based Deep Learning Radiomics Nomogram for the Survival Prediction in Gallbladder Cancer.
Clinical_Task: Prognostic: 1-year overall survival after surgical resection
Texture_Families: Shape, First-order, GLCM, GLRLM, GLSZM, GLDM, NGTDM (incl. wavelet/LoG/LBP filters)
Stat_Comparison: DeLong p=0.298 (Rad vs DTL); DLR Nomogram vs Rad DeLong p=0.127 (NS)
Notes: DTL Sig from largest 2D slice via DenseNet121; Rad best=RF; integrated DLR nomogram AUC=0.870, C-index=0.736 in test; Center-based split: Center 2 trained n=105, Center 1 tested n=62 (per Methods + Fig 2); RECONCILED Phase 4a->ExtVal=YES on May 19 2026; AUTHORS' OWN DISCUSSION calls this 'lacked external test cohorts' (likely treating sister-center within Shanxi Medical Univ network as internal). Strict PROBAST/RQS reading: external single-site (+3). Phase 5 sensitivity analysis may want to treat this as a borderline 'within-network external'. FIRST PAPER IN CORPUS WHERE RADIOMICS > FM (Delta = -0.084, NS p=0.298)

Title: An MRI-Based Deep Transfer Learning Radiomics Nomogram to Predict Ki-67 Proliferation Index of Meningioma.
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: Ki-67 proliferation index (high >=5% vs low <5%)
Texture_Families: NR (standard PyRadiomics; specific families not enumerated)
Notes: Single-center, enhanced T1WI only; 2D max-ROI input to ResNet50; DTLR nomogram (clinical+Rad+DTL) AUC=0.779; clinical-only AUC=0.746; no external val; SECOND PAPER IN CORPUS WHERE RADIOMICS > FM (Delta = -0.033, NS p=0.5977)

Title: Integrating radiomics and deep learning for enhanced prediction of high-grade patterns in stage IA lung adenocarcinoma.
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: presence of high-grade patterns (HGP>=5%) in stage IA invasive adenocarcinoma
FM_Usage_Mode: Frozen feature extractor (penultimate Avgpool layer + PCA to 256)
Texture_Families: Shape, First-order, GLCM, GLRLM, GLSZM, GLDM, NGTDM (incl. wavelet/LoG/LBP/square/squareroot/exponential/gradient filters)
Stat_Comparison: DeLong p=0.59 (DLR vs DL, test); DLR vs Rad p<0.001 train
Notes: RECONCILED 2026-05-19: per Methods (Taizhou Hospital of Zhejiang Province, Jan 2023-Jan 2024), 213=Train (Jan-Oct 2023, 7:3 split with val), 92=Internal Validation/tuning cohort (Jan-Oct 2023, classifier selection via 5-fold CV + Gridsearch -- folded into Notes since schema lacks Cohort_Internal_Val column), 98=Internal Test (temporal hold-out Nov 2023-Jan 2024, deliberately enriched with 32 thick-slice 2.5mm CTs transferred from train/val for stress-testing auto-segmentation). Single-center confirmed in Limitations. Two CT scanners (GE Discover CT750 HD, UCT 530) at one site = scanner diversity, NOT multi-center. RQS Item 12 stays +2 (internal). External_Validation=NO is correct (same site, same scanners, different time window only).

Title: Integrating manual annotation with deep transfer learning and radiomics for vertebral fracture analysis.
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: acute vs chronic vertebral compression fracture
Texture_Families: First-order, Shape, GLCM, GLSZM, GLRLM, NGTDM, GLDM (incl. LoG, Wavelet, LBP3D, Exponential, Square, SquareRoot, Logarithm, Gradient filters)
Stat_Comparison: DeLong NS (fusion vs single arms); DCA favored fusion
Data_Available: Available from corresponding author on reasonable request
Notes: Single-center; 6 DTL arms tested with ROI expansions (0/2/4/6/8/10 voxels), 6-voxel optimal; fusion model AUC=0.964 (test); ShuffleNet layer3.3.conv3 features reduced to 18 dims; Stat_Comparison reports fusion-vs-single-arm contrasts, NOT direct FM-vs-Rad DeLong test

Title: Novel CT radiomics models for the postoperative prediction of early recurrence of resectable pancreatic adenocarcinoma: A single-center retrospective study in China.
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: early recurrence (within 9 months) after resection of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
Texture_Families: Shape, First-order, GLCM, GLRLM, GLSZM, GLDM, NGTDM
Notes: Single-center, resectable PDAC only; 512 deep features reduced; clinical AUC=0.640, pTNM AUC=0.673; DCA & NRI/IDI favor deep-radiomics; no external val; MedicalNet = 3D medical-image domain-pretrained ResNet50 (NOT ImageNet-CNN tier)

Title: Integrating multimodal ultrasound imaging for improved radiomics sentinel lymph node assessment in breast cancer.
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: sentinel lymph node metastasis (N0 vs N>=1) in breast cancer
Texture_Families: First-order, Shape (2D), GLCM, GLRLM, GLSZM, GLDM, NGTDM (incl. wavelet/DWT filters)
Stat_Comparison: DeLong p=0.029 (COMB vs ONLY_DF); p=0.031 (COMB vs ONLY_HF)
Notes: Dual-branch MobileNetV2 fuses BMUS+CDUS at block 7; 2048 deep feats->PCA 256; COMB (6 HF+4 DF) AUC=0.837 test; external val from 2nd hospital n=105; also 3-class N0/N1-2/N>=3 task; THIRD PAPER IN CORPUS WHERE RADIOMICS > FM (Delta = -0.022); FIRST ULTRASOUND PAPER; FIRST EXT-VAL IN ULTRASOUND

Title: Development and Validation of a Feature-Based Broad-Learning System for Opportunistic Osteoporosis Screening Using Lumbar Spine Radiographs.
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: osteoporosis vs non-osteoporosis
Notes: 3-center; train Philips DR/test Siemens DR; VGG11+BLS test AUC=0.632 (DeLong p=0.002 vs RF); pretrained CNNs as frozen feature extractors; women >=50y; SMOTE applied; FOURTH PAPER WHERE RADIOMICS > FM AND FIRST STATISTICALLY SIGNIFICANT Rad-advantage in corpus (Delta = -0.148, p=0.002); Note: reported DeLong is for VGG11-vs-RF; ResNet18-vs-RF test value not separately stated but corpus-primary FM arm is ResNet18 (0.654) per best-of-FM rule RECONCILED 2026-05-19: Strict reading applied. Methods §Lumbar Spine Radiographic Protocol states 'training set used Philips DigitalDiagnost DR; test set used Siemens AXIOM AristosVX DR (respectively)' = scanner-vendor segregation but no explicit center allocation. Paper never names which of 3 centers go where. PROBAST/TRIPOD require explicit institutional separation -> ExtVal stays NO. RQS Item 12 downgraded from +3 to +2 (internal) -> RQS total 6->5 (NEW CORPUS LOW). RQS Item 5=-3 confirmed: NO feature reduction (LASSO/Pearson/mRMR/ICC), NO multiple-testing correction (Bonferroni/FDR/BH); BLS random-projection EXPANDS dimensionality from ~85 features to 872 derived nodes -- contradicts the RQS-Item-5 intent.

Title: Ultrasound-based deep learning radiomics model for differentiating benign, borderline, and malignant ovarian tumours: a multi-class classification exploratory study.
Clinical_Task: Multiclass classification: benign vs borderline vs malignant ovarian tumour
Texture_Families: First-order, Shape, GLCM, GLDM, GLRLM, GLSZM
Notes: Micro-avg AUC test; DTL_Sig macro-avg=0.85 (benign 0.87/BOT 0.82/malig 0.84); combined DLR_Sig micro=0.90/macro=0.84; Rad_Sig and Clinic_Sig failed to recognize BOT class (clinically important: radiomics-only model could not classify the borderline-tumour class at all, while FM did); FIRST MULTI-CLASS TASK IN CORPUS; SECOND ULTRASOUND PAPER

Title: Deep learning nomogram for predicting neoadjuvant chemotherapy response in locally advanced gastric cancer patients.
Clinical_Task: Treatment response: NAC response (TRG-based GR vs PR) in locally advanced gastric cancer
Texture_Families: First-order, Shape, GLCM, GLRLM, GLSZM, GLDM, NGTDM (s-order)
Notes: Multi-phase CECT (arterial/portal/delayed); internal val AUCs reported; external val DL=0.731 vs Rad=0.536; combined nomogram (DL+Rad+cT) AUC 0.802/0.751; LARGEST POSITIVE DELTA IN CORPUS (+0.227); but driven by weak radiomics arm (Rad AUC=0.559 ~chance on internal, 0.536 on external) — sensitivity analysis flag for Phase 5

Title: Multi-feature Fusion Network on Gray Scale Ultrasonography: Effective Differentiation of Adenolymphoma and Pleomorphic Adenoma.
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: pleomorphic adenoma vs adenolymphoma
Notes: Internal val AUCs; external val DTL=0.825 vs Rad=0.748; fine-tuned then frozen final FC (2048->PCA 128); DLR=0.863/0.859; DLRN (with clinical)=0.908/0.908; tested DenseNet169/Inception_V3/VGG16 (ResNet50 best); FIFTH PAPER WHERE RADIOMICS > FM and SECOND STAT SIG Rad advantage (after Paper 14); SIGN-FLIP between internal (Rad wins) and external (FM wins by 0.077) — methodologically important for cross-cohort generalization discussion

Title: Multiparametric MRI-based radiomics approach with deep transfer learning for preoperative prediction of Ki-67 status in sinonasal squamous cell carcinoma.
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: high vs low Ki-67 expression (cutoff 50%) in sinonasal SCC
Stat_Comparison: Training: DLR>DTL,HC p<0.050; Test: DLR vs HC NS
Notes: Multiparametric (FS-T2WI/CE-T1WI/ADC); ResNet50 2048->PCA 96; fused DLR-RS AUC=0.817 (best); training AUCs HC=0.882/DTL=0.987/DLR=1.000 (DTL overfit -- training 0.987 vs test 0.650 indicates classic ImageNet-CNN small-cohort overfitting); SIXTH PAPER WHERE RADIOMICS > FM and LARGEST NEGATIVE DELTA IN CORPUS (-0.153); ImageNet-CNN tier; stat tests reported are DLR fusion contrasts, NOT direct FM-vs-Rad

Title: Ultrasound-based deep learning radiomics nomogram for differentiating mass mastitis from invasive breast cancer.
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: mass mastitis vs invasive breast cancer (BI-RADS 4)
FM_Usage_Mode: Frozen feature extractor (transfer-learning fine-tuned then fixed)
Stat_Comparison: DeLong NS for RF (validation); training RF DL vs RAD p=0.01
Notes: Single-center; 50 MM vs 180 IBC; RF best across all; DLR-nomogram (RF) AUC=0.90 train/0.90 val (NRI +61% vs DL p=0.03); unsupervised k-means confirmed phenotype separation; direct FM-vs-Rad DeLong on test = NS

Title: Differentiation of acute and chronic vertebral compression fractures using conventional CT based on deep transfer learning features and hand-crafted radiomics features.
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: acute vs chronic vertebral compression fractures
Stat_Comparison: DeLong test (significant for most pairs p<0.05; nomogram vs fusion NS p=0.668)
Notes: Test cohort AUCs; MRI bone marrow edema as gold standard; 50 DTL + 41 HCR -> 74 fused features; fusion model AUC=0.915, DLR-nomogram (+age/sex) AUC=0.946; SAME TASK AS PAPER 11 (acute vs chronic VCF) -- two ImageNet-CNN papers on identical task: Paper 11 ShuffleNet Delta=+0.072 NS, Paper 20 ResNet50 Delta=+0.017 likely-NS; specific FM-vs-Rad DeLong p value not stated, only summary 'most pairs sig'

Title: Novel Multiparametric Magnetic Resonance Imaging-Based Deep Learning and Clinical Parameter Integration for the Prediction of Long-Term Biochemical Recurrence-Free Survival in Prostate Cancer after Radical Prostatectomy.
Clinical_Task: Prognostic: 10-year biochemical recurrence-free survival after radical prostatectomy
Notes: 5-fold CV on n=437 (110 BCR); 72 features/modality x 3 (T2WI/ADC/DCE)=216; best overall = CDLM-Deep feature (FM+clinical) C-index=0.89, iAUC=0.93, HR=7.72; CRM-Multi C-index=0.83; CM clinical-only C-index=0.81; DLM HR=4.37; RM-Multi HR=2.67; single-center Korean cohort; 2.5D EfficientNet-B0, 45 frozen+158 fine-tuned layers, 320 deep features/modality; cohort sizes ~350/~87 are approximations from 5-fold CV on n=437; CLINICAL-ONLY MODEL (0.81) BEATS BOTH IMAGING ARMS -- worth flagging in Discussion: imaging predictors are secondary to clinical in this task

Title: Deep learning-based radiomic nomogram to predict risk categorization of thymic epithelial tumors: A multicenter study.
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: low-risk vs high-risk TET histological categorization (WHO types A/AB/B1 vs B2/B3/carcinoma)
Notes: 5-fold CV on derivation (628); pure DL_Sig external AUC=0.724 vs Rad_Sig external 0.716; fusion DLR_Sig (handcrafted+DL) AUC=0.883 int / 0.749 ext; best DLRN (DLR_Sig+age+sex, logistic regression) AUC=0.965 int / 0.786 ext; ResNet50 2048-D feats from avg-pool, PCA->32D; largest tumor slice; 3 hospitals, China; RQS=22 (self-reported, 61%); HUGE INTERNAL/EXTERNAL GAP: internal Delta=+0.133 collapses to external Delta=+0.008 (effectively tied) -- second sign-flip-style pattern after Paper 17

Title: A deep learning radiomics model may help to improve the prediction performance of preoperative grading in meningioma.
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: low-grade (WHO I) vs high-grade (WHO II/III) meningioma
Notes: Single-center retrospective, n=132 (84 low/48 high); contrast-enhanced T1WI axial+sagittal; 1688 radiomics features x 2 sequences = 3376; 2048 DL features x 2 sequences = 4096; pure DLM AUC=0.718 (SMOTE) inferior to RM AUC=0.918; best model is fused DLRM (handcrafted+DL) AUC=0.935 test / 0.988 train w/ SMOTE; 15 final features (12 radiomics + 3 DL); SMOTE for class imbalance; ICC>=0.90 filter; ANOVA+LightGBM importance+RFECV selection; LARGEST NEGATIVE DELTA IN CORPUS (-0.200); SEVENTH Rad-wins paper; ResNeXt101 has ~88M params -- classic small-cohort overfit pattern (n=132)

Title: Computed tomography-based deep-learning prediction of neoadjuvant chemoradiotherapy treatment response in esophageal squamous cell carcinoma.
Clinical_Task: Treatment response: predicting pathologic complete response to neoadjuvant chemoradiotherapy in ESCC
Notes: 2-center retrospective (SYSUCC train n=161 / HKU test n=70); pCR rates 46.0% vs 44.3%; 6 CNNs compared (Xception 0.763, VGG16 0.648, VGG19 0.635, ResNet50 0.805, InceptionV3 0.753, IncResNetV2 0.653); 14 DL features after RFA selection; ComBat harmonization; 3-slice ROI bounding box 224x224; clinical model AUC=0.508; whole-volume radiomics AUC=0.712; fused DL+radiomics AUC=0.799 (no gain over DL alone); radiogenomics: WNT/TGF-beta/ECM; RECONCILED 2026-05-19: Rad_Arm_Value=0.725 is the PRIMARY radiomics arm (3-slice ROI matched to DL input geometry, per Methods §ROIs). The 0.712 figure is the SECONDARY radiomics arm (whole-volume ROI), described as 'comparison' in Methods and 'remained similar' in Results. Delta=+0.080 is correct (head-to-head matched-input comparison). The 0.712 is preserved here for audit-trail completeness but should NOT be used as the primary value.; model-zoo selection of best CNN is RQS-flaggable; data sharing is genomics-only, NOT imaging

Title: Transfer learning radiomic model predicts intratumoral tertiary lymphoid structures in hepatocellular carcinoma: a multicenter study.
Source: Stage 3 re-evaluation — MAYBE upgraded to INCLUDE
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: predicting intratumoral tertiary lymphoid structures (iTLS) status in HCC
Data_Available: MRI: NR (restricted); RNA-seq: TCGA-LIHC; TCIA cohort: https://zenodo.org/records/8179129
Notes: 4-center retrospective n=660 (308 iTLS+); multiphase MRI (T2WI+AP+PVP); TL features: 19 selected; Rad features: 13 selected (total extracted NR); ComBat-style preprocessing (N4 bias); fused TLR model best: AUC=0.91 train / 0.85 internal / 0.85 external; pure TL ext AUC=0.78; CR model AUC=0.60; TLR linked to OS/RFS; 101-pt combo-therapy set: PR/CR 36% vs 13.7% (p=0.009); ImageNet-CNN tier with stat-sig FM advantage (DeLong p<0.01) and PROPER external validation -- second such paper after Paper 16; texture_families and N_features radiomics NR for the radiomics arm

Title: Prediction of prognosis in acute ischemic stroke after mechanical thrombectomy based on multimodal MRI radiomics and deep learning.
Source: Stage 3 re-evaluation — MAYBE upgraded to INCLUDE
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: poor prognosis (mRS 3-6) at discharge post-thrombectomy
Stat_Comparison: DeLong p=0.513 (DL vs Rad, validation cohort)
Notes: Single-center retrospective, no external validation. Combined CRD model best (AUC 0.908 val/0.834 train). 2.5D CNN (slices n-2,n,n+2). Mixed 1.5T/3T scanners. DeLong DL vs Rad train p=0.242. Radiomics > DL here; RECONCILED 2026-05-19: Methods §2.4 Deep Learning Procedure confirms transfer learning but does NOT name the pretraining corpus (no mention of ImageNet/MedicalNet/RadImageNet/Med3D anywhere in paper). Pragmatic inference applied: ResNet101 + PyTorch + 'transfer learning' with no cited corpus is almost certainly torchvision.models default (ImageNet1k); MedicalNet/RadImageNet are non-default and would require explicit citation. Coded as 'ImageNet (inferred -- not explicitly stated)'. Tier classification = ImageNet-CNN (unchanged from working assumption). §Limitations flag: 'Pretraining corpus not reported in N papers despite explicit use of transfer learning; PyTorch ImageNet default presumed but not coded.' EIGHTH PAPER WHERE RADIOMICS > FM with direct DeLong test reported NS

Title: Development and validation of a prediction model for lymph node metastasis in thyroid cancer: integrating deep learning and radiomics features from intra- and peri-tumoral regions.
Source: Stage 3 re-evaluation — MAYBE upgraded to INCLUDE
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: central lymph node metastasis (CLNM) in PTC
Texture_Families: Shape, first-order, texture, wavelet (GLCM/GLRLM/GLSZM/etc. not individually specified)
Stat_Comparison: DeLong p=0.17 (intra+peri DL vs intra+peri RAD+DL fusion; intra+peri DL vs intra+peri RAD not directly reported)
Notes: Best intra+peri DL AUC 0.846 internal/0.853 external; intra+peri RAD 0.828/0.821; fusion RAD+DL best AUC 0.897/0.881. Peri-tumoral ROI = 3-voxel expansion; intra+peri region design; FIRST THYROID PAPER IN CORPUS; FIFTH ULTRASOUND PAPER; direct FM-vs-Rad DeLong p value NOT reported (wrong contrast)

Title: A fusion model integrating magnetic resonance imaging radiomics and deep learning features for predicting alpha-thalassemia X-linked intellectual disability mutation status in isocitrate dehydrogenase-mutant high-grade astrocytoma: a multicenter study.
Source: Stage 3 re-evaluation — MAYBE upgraded to INCLUDE
Clinical_Task: Binary classification: ATRX mutation status in IDH-mutant high-grade astrocytoma
Stat_Comparison: DeLong p=0.857 (overall DL vs overall radiomics, test set)
Notes: Overall lesion ROI best; FM also tested on edema (AUC 0.775) and tumor (0.887) ROIs. Hybrid RAD+DL fusion AUC 0.936 test; full clinical fusion 0.949; NINTH PAPER WHERE RADIOMICS > FM, statistically tied (DeLong p=0.857); FINAL PAPER OF PHASE 4a EXTRACTION
